description_long: This object is a stylized flower with broad, elongated petals arranged in a radial pattern, each petal displaying a gradient of purple hues with subtle blue undertones. The petals are slightly curved and taper to pointed tips, with small, irregular holes distributed along their surfaces, giving a delicate and somewhat weathered appearance. The center of the flower is dark and recessed, forming a subtle focal point from which the petals radiate outward. The overall structure is low and flat, with the petals spreading widely and nearly parallel to the ground. No stem or leaves are visible, and the flower appears to rest directly on a surface. The coloration and form suggest an ornamental or fantastical species, suitable for decorative use in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A low, wide, purple flower with elongated, slightly curved petals radiating from a dark center and featuring small holes along the petal surfaces. The flower has a stylized, ornamental appearance with no visible stem or leaves.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from above, showing the full radial arrangement of the purple petals and the dark central area.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the flat, low profile of the flower and the gentle upward curve of the petals.
description_view_2: Another side angle, showing the petal arrangement and the subtle gradient from purple to blue across the petals.
description_view_3: A rotated side view, emphasizing the pointed petal tips and the irregular holes along the petal surfaces.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, radiating symmetrically from a central point. Each petal slightly overlaps its neighbor, forming a circular, layered pattern typical of many ornamental flowers.
